cta.title: Wondering If We're the Right Fit for You?
cta.description: Reach out to book a complimentary consultation and talk through your personal health concerns with our team.

hero.title: Health Conditions We Support
hero.subtitle: From everyday discomfort to complex chronic concerns, Traditional Chinese Medicine provides natural, evidence-informed approaches for a broad spectrum of health conditions.

categories.name: Pain Management
categories.subtitle: Compassionate, drug-free relief for both acute injuries and long-term pain conditions
categories.description: Acupuncture and TCM therapies address lower back pain, upper back pain, and sciatica — whether your symptoms came on suddenly or have persisted over time — all without relying on medications. - Qi stagnation and blood stasis are common TCM explanations for neck and shoulder pain. - Acupuncture is used to release trigger points and ease localized tension. - Tui Na helps relax tight muscles, while cupping supports improved circulation throughout the affected area.

categories.name: Mental & Emotional Wellbeing
categories.description: Caring support for stress, anxiety, depression, and overall emotional health

categories.name: Digestive Health
categories.description: Targeted care for digestive disorders and long-term gut health

categories.name: Women's Health
categories.description: Thoughtful, whole-person care addressing a wide range of women's health needs

categories.name: Respiratory Issues
categories.description: Gentle support for the respiratory system and healthy breathing

categories.name: Immune System
categories.description: Build resilience and support your body's natural ability to defend itself

conditions.title: Back Pain
conditions.description: Acupuncture and TCM therapies offer meaningful relief for lower back pain, upper back pain, and sciatica — addressing both sudden-onset and long-standing symptoms without the use of drugs or surgery.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, back pain is understood as a disruption of Qi and blood flow through the body's channels. Our practitioners apply acupuncture to address these blockages, cupping to support healthy circulation, and herbal medicine to help reduce inflammation and build strength in the back.

conditions.title: Neck & Shoulder Pain
conditions.description: Tension, stiffness, and aching in the neck and shoulders — whether rooted in stress, postural habits, or an injury — can be addressed with targeted, natural therapies.
conditions.tcmApproach: From a TCM perspective, neck and shoulder pain frequently stems from Qi stagnation and blood stasis in the affected channels. We use acupuncture to release trigger points, Tui Na to ease muscle tightness, and cupping to promote better circulation.

conditions.title: Arthritis & Joint Pain
symptoms: Pain that intensifies with changes in weather
conditions.description: Natural approaches to managing pain associated with osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, and general joint inflammation.
conditions.tcmApproach: TCM classifies arthritis under 'Bi Syndrome,' a pattern in which pathogenic factors — wind, cold, dampness, or heat — obstruct the joints. Care is directed at clearing these factors, calming inflammation, and supporting the body's overall strength and resilience.

conditions.title: Headaches & Migraines
conditions.description: For tension headaches, migraines, and persistent head pain, we offer a medication-free path to meaningful relief.
conditions.tcmApproach: TCM recognizes several distinct headache patterns — among them Liver Yang rising, Blood deficiency, and external pathogenic influences. By identifying which pattern applies to you, we tailor a treatment plan using acupuncture and herbal medicine to address the root cause.

conditions.title: Anxiety & Panic Disorders
conditions.description: Drug-free, natural support for those living with anxiety, panic attacks, and persistent nervousness.
conditions.tcmApproach: Anxiety is frequently linked in TCM to Heart and Kidney disharmony, Liver Qi stagnation, or disturbance of the Shen (spirit). Treatment works to calm the Shen, restore the smooth flow of Qi, and nourish the underlying organ systems to bring emotional balance back into equilibrium.

conditions.title: Depression & Low Mood
symptoms: Trouble with focus and mental clarity
conditions.description: Whole-person support for those experiencing depression, depleted energy, and a loss of motivation.
conditions.tcmApproach: Depression in TCM is often associated with Liver Qi stagnation, Heart Blood deficiency, or Spleen Qi deficiency. Treatment centers on moving stagnant Qi, addressing underlying deficiencies, and gently supporting an uplift in mood and vitality.

conditions.title: Insomnia & Sleep Disorders
symptoms: Trouble getting to sleep at night
conditions.description: Supportive, natural care for those who struggle to fall asleep, wake frequently through the night, or experience consistently poor sleep quality.
conditions.tcmApproach: From a TCM perspective, disrupted sleep frequently traces back to imbalances between the Heart and Kidney, Yin deficiency generating internal heat, or disturbance of the Shen. Our approach focuses on nourishing Yin, settling the Heart, and anchoring the Shen so that genuinely restorative sleep becomes possible.

conditions.title: Stress & Burnout
conditions.description: Rebuild balance and inner resilience when prolonged stress or burnout has taken a toll on your wellbeing.
conditions.tcmApproach: Over time, chronic stress drains Qi and leads to Liver stagnation throughout the body. We work to move and regulate Liver Qi, address underlying deficiencies, and introduce practical stress management strategies — helping you rebuild balance and develop lasting resilience.

conditions.title: IBS & Digestive Issues
conditions.description: Targeted support for irritable bowel syndrome, bloating, gas, and everyday digestive discomfort.
conditions.tcmApproach: IBS commonly reflects a disharmony between the Liver and Spleen, where emotional stress interferes directly with digestive function. We address this by moving Liver Qi, fortifying Spleen function, and bringing the digestive system back into balance.

conditions.title: Acid Reflux & GERD
conditions.description: Supportive care for heartburn, acid reflux, and gastroesophageal reflux disease using a natural TCM approach.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, reflux is understood as Stomach Qi moving in the wrong direction — often accompanied by heat or phlegm. Treatment aims to redirect Stomach Qi downward, clear any associated heat, and support overall digestive strength.

conditions.title: Constipation
conditions.description: Thoughtful, effective support for chronic constipation and unpredictable bowel patterns.
conditions.tcmApproach: Constipation can arise from several distinct TCM patterns — including Qi stagnation, Blood deficiency, Yin deficiency, or Spleen Qi weakness. Because the root cause varies from person to person, treatment is tailored to your individual pattern with the goal of restoring healthy bowel function.

conditions.title: Fertility Support
conditions.description: Whole-person support for those pursuing natural conception or preparing for IVF — for both women and men.
conditions.tcmApproach: Our care centers on regulating menstrual cycles, nourishing Kidney Essence (Jing), encouraging healthy blood flow to the reproductive organs, and easing stress. Each partner's treatment plan is shaped around their own unique needs and patterns.

conditions.title: Menstrual Disorders
conditions.description: Supportive natural care for painful periods, irregular cycles, PMS, and a range of menstrual-related concerns.
conditions.tcmApproach: Menstrual concerns frequently involve Liver Qi stagnation, Blood deficiency, or Cold lodged in the uterus. We work to regulate the cycle, nourish and move Blood, support Qi flow, and clear pathogenic factors that may be contributing to symptoms.

conditions.title: Menopause Symptoms
conditions.description: Compassionate, natural support for hot flashes, night sweats, mood shifts, and other symptoms of the menopausal transition.
conditions.tcmApproach: Menopause reflects a natural decline in both Kidney Yin and Yang. To ease this transition and reduce associated symptoms, we nourish Kidney Yin, bring Yin and Yang into greater harmony, and calm the Shen.

conditions.title: PCOS (Polycystic Ovary Syndrome)
symptoms: Irregular periods or absence of menstruation
conditions.description: A whole-person approach to supporting hormonal balance, restoring regular cycles, and managing the symptoms of PCOS.
conditions.tcmApproach: PCOS typically presents with a combination of Kidney deficiency, Phlegm-Damp accumulation, and Liver Qi stagnation. Treatment prioritizes resolving phlegm, moving and regulating Qi, and strengthening Kidney function to support overall balance.

conditions.title: Allergies & Hay Fever
conditions.description: Natural, supportive care for seasonal allergies, hay fever, and sensitivities to environmental triggers.
conditions.tcmApproach: Allergic responses often point to weakness in Wei Qi (the body's defensive Qi) alongside internal imbalances. Our approach strengthens Wei Qi, clears pathogenic influences, and works to calm inflammatory reactions.

conditions.title: Asthma
conditions.description: Complementary TCM support for asthma management, aimed at reducing how often and how severely episodes occur.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, asthma is associated with deficiency in both the Lung and Kidney, combined with phlegm accumulation in the airways. Treatment focuses on building Lung and Kidney strength, resolving phlegm, and lowering overall reactivity.

conditions.title: Chronic Cough
conditions.description: Care for a persistent cough that has continued despite conventional treatment approaches.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, chronic cough can reflect several distinct patterns — among them Lung Yin deficiency, Lung Heat, or Phlegm accumulation. By identifying which pattern is present, we tailor treatment to address your specific underlying imbalance.

conditions.title: Frequent Colds & Flu
conditions.description: Support your immune system to help lower how often you get sick and reduce the impact of colds and respiratory infections when they do occur.
conditions.tcmApproach: Getting sick often can point to Wei Qi deficiency in TCM. Our approach focuses on strengthening Wei Qi, supporting Lung and Spleen function, and building up the body's overall immune resilience over time.

conditions.title: Chronic Fatigue
symptoms: Struggling to get through everyday tasks
conditions.description: When persistent tiredness and low energy interfere with daily life, our care focuses on supporting the body's natural vitality and helping restore a sense of wellbeing.
conditions.tcmApproach: From a TCM perspective, chronic fatigue often points to Qi deficiency in the Spleen, Kidneys, or both — and may also involve Blood deficiency. Treatment is directed at addressing these deficiencies and helping the body rebuild its energy and vitality.
treatmentMethods: Guidance on balancing rest and physical activity

introduction.note: Not finding your condition on this list? Reach out to us — there's a good chance we can still support you.
introduction.text: At Dr. Henry Smith Clinic, care is centered on the whole person — not simply the symptoms. We take a comprehensive approach that looks for the root causes of illness while also addressing immediate discomfort. The conditions listed below represent some of what we commonly support through our practice.
